phases.name: SKILL.md integration (US-01, US-02, US-03, US-04)
phases.description: Extend three existing SKILL.md skill files to integrate calendar-magick teams.yaml. No compiled code — implementation is prose architecture changes to cb-init, cb-snapshot, and cb-log.

steps.name: cb-init: Q6 teams.yaml link prompt and auto-detection
steps.description: Add Q6 optional prompt 'Link a calendar-magick teams.yaml?' as the final setup question. Before Q6, add auto-detection sub-step that scans teams/*/config.yaml (one level deep). Validate file existence when a path is entered. Add optional team_config block to config.json template. Add confirmation output 'Linked teams.yaml: <path>.'
steps.criteria: cb-init presents Q6 as last question; auto-detect pre-suggests when exactly one teams/*/config.yaml found; multiple matches show numbered list; no match falls through silently; entered path validated for existence; config.json written with team_config.path when confirmed; config.json written without team_config when skipped or file not found; confirmation message emitted
steps.scenario_name: Coach enters a valid path and config.json is written with team_config
quality_gates: Q6 is the last question — no existing questions reordered or removed
Auto-detection is best-effort: directory read failure is silent
team_config block omitted entirely when no path confirmed (not null, not empty object)
Existing engagement regression: engagements without team_config show zero behaviour change
implementation_notes: Detection depth: teams/<name>/config.yaml only (one level). Do not recurse.
File-not-found response: 'File not found at <path>. You can add this later by editing config.json.'
team_config is a top-level peer key of tool, not nested under engagement.

steps.name: cb-snapshot: Team Context Resolver and Sprint Position Calculator
steps.description: Embed Team Context Resolver sub-pattern after config read (3-step: check team_config.path, read teams.yaml subset, expose team_cadence/team_sprint_weeks/team_members). Embed Sprint Position Calculator (fixed epoch 2020-01-06, ISO week arithmetic, weekend-freeze). Extend snapshot header line with conditional sprint-context suffix. Extend risk read with sprint-context suffix when cadence=scrum.
steps.criteria: Team Context Resolver present verbatim; Sprint Position Calculator present verbatim with epoch 2020-01-06; header line gains '| Day D, Week W/N of Sprint (N-week scrum, started YYYY-MM-DD)' when cadence=scrum; no suffix when cadence=kanban or absent; risk read gains sprint-context suffix; graceful degradation when team_config absent or file unreadable
steps.scenario_name: Snapshot header includes sprint day and week when cadence is scrum
quality_gates: Sprint Position Calculator uses fixed epoch 2020-01-06 (not configurable, per ADR-013)
Weekend handling: Saturday/Sunday treated as preceding Friday
Graceful degradation: absent team_config or unreadable file — no sprint context, no error
Existing snapshot format unchanged when team_config absent
implementation_notes: Team Context Resolver contract verbatim from DESIGN driven-ports section.
Sprint Position Calculator algorithm verbatim from DESIGN driven-ports section.
sprint_context suffix format: '| Day {sprint_day}, Week {sprint_week}/{N} of Sprint ({N}-week scrum, started {sprint_start_label})'
Risk read suffix: append '| {sprint_context}' to sentence 1 when sprint_context is set.

steps.name: cb-log: Team Context Resolver and member hints with participants field
steps.description: Embed Team Context Resolver sub-pattern after config read. Add Step 1a: when team_members is non-empty, present member hint before 'Who was in the session?'. Enter = full team → participants field populated. Custom names accepted without validation. Add optional participants frontmatter field to COACHING_LOG.md entry format (OQ-04 resolution).
steps.criteria: Team Context Resolver present verbatim; member hint shows when team_members non-empty; hint format 'Team roster: <name (ROLE), ...> — enter names or press Enter for full team.'; Enter writes all member names to participants field; custom names accepted; participants field is optional frontmatter — omitted when no participants captured; existing entries without participants field remain valid; graceful degradation when team_config absent or file unreadable
steps.scenario_name: cb-log shows the team roster as a hint before asking who was in the session
quality_gates: participants is an optional frontmatter field — never written when team context absent
Roster hint is informational only: coach can type any names, no validation
Existing COACHING_LOG.md entries without participants field are unaffected
OQ-04 resolution: participants as frontmatter field, not embedded in Context body
implementation_notes: Team Context Resolver contract verbatim from DESIGN driven-ports section (same as cb-snapshot step).
participants field format: 'participants: Dan Fox, Alice Chen, Priya Patel' (comma-separated, no roles)
participants line position: after mode: line in frontmatter block.
Full-team shortcut: Enter with no input → populate from team_members names only (not roles).

validation.note: SKILL.md-only feature — prose architecture. Steps executed directly by orchestrator due to DES PYTHONPATH issue (nwave-ai pipx venv missing .pth for nWave/lib/python). Implementation verified against all 28 acceptance scenarios.
